title: Kth Largest Element in an Array
description:
Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the kth largest element in the array.

Example:
Input:
nums = [3,2,1,5,6,4]
k = 2

Output:
5
hint: A min heap can keep track of the k largest elements while iterating through the array.

title: Last Stone Weight
description:
You are given an array of stones where stones[i] represents the weight of a stone.

Repeatedly smash the two heaviest stones together. If they have different weights, the remaining stone has the difference between their weights.

Return the weight of the last remaining stone, or 0 if none remain.

Example:
Input:
[2,7,4,1,8,1]

Output:
1
hint: Use a max heap to quickly access the two heaviest stones.

title: Find Median from Data Stream
description:
Design a data structure that supports adding numbers and finding the median of all numbers added so far.

Example:
addNum(1)
addNum(2)
findMedian()
Output: 1.5
hint: Use two heaps: a max heap for the smaller half and a min heap for the larger half.

title: IPO Maximum Capital
description:
Given projects with profits and required capital, maximize your final capital after completing k projects.

Example:
Input:
k=2
profits=[1,2,3]
capital=[0,1,1]

Output:
4
hint: Use a heap to always choose the most profitable available project.
